name: agentseal
description: Security toolkit for AI agents. Scan your machine for dangerous skills and MCP configs, monitor for supply chain attacks, test prompt injection resistance, and audit live MCP servers for tool poisoning.

commit.message:
Add quick links, Code of Conduct, and Security Policy

- Quick links bar under tagline (Website, Docs, Quick Start, SealBench, Twitter)
- CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md for community guidelines
- SECURITY.md for responsible vulnerability disclosure
Fix shield test: remove recursive watch (unsupported on Node 18 Linux)

Fix flaky shield watcher test in CI

Add 200ms delay for watcher initialization and increase timeout to 5s.
fs.watch can miss early events on Linux runners.
Add GitHub repo security analysis as Pro feature across all READMEs

Rewrite all three READMEs - GitHub, PyPI, npm

- GitHub README: 4-pillar structure (guard/shield/scan/scan-mcp), AgentSeal Repo badge, collapsible FAQ, scan-mcp as top-level command
- PyPI README: simplified for package page, focused on Python API and CLI usage
- npm README: updated probe counts (191), added scan-mcp/guard/shield references, cleaner structure

Port full Python agentseal to TypeScript — 729 tests, 10 waves complete

Wave A: deobfuscate.ts — 11 deobfuscation functions (zero-width, base64, bidi, etc.)
Wave B: guard-models.ts, skill-scanner.ts, blocklist.ts — pattern matching, 9 rules
Wave C: machine-discovery.ts — 27 AI agent configs, scanMachine/scanDirectory, JSONC
Wave D: guard.ts — Guard class orchestrating full pipeline
Wave E: mcp-checker.ts — MCPConfigChecker, 10 security checks (MCP-001 to CVEs)
Wave F: toxic-flows.ts, baselines.ts — 107 server labels, BaselineStore rug pull detection
Wave G: shield.ts, notify.ts — continuous fs monitoring with debounced scanning
Wave H: profiles.ts, probes/loader.ts — 8 scan profiles, JSON/YAML probe loader
Wave I: fix.ts — quarantine/restore, report I/O, fixable skill extraction
Wave J: chains.ts, llm-judge.ts — attack chain detection, BYOK LLM analysis

Wave 1 hardening + Wave 2 scan-mcp: complete security engine

Wave 1 — Gap closures & quality fixes:
- AST-based dataflow analysis: credential→network taint tracking (Python + JS)
- Unicode invisible char detection: 6 categories (zero-width, tags, BiDi, selectors)
- OWASP MCP-007 to MCP-012: command injection, oversharing, supply chain checks
- 30+ credential patterns with high-entropy secret detection
- CVE checks: CVE-2025-53110, CVE-2025-68143, CVE-2025-59536, CVE-2025-6514
- CVE-2025-68143 now detects npx-based git servers (server-git, mcp-git in args)
- Guard --path flag for targeted directory scanning
- SARIF + HTML output modes (banner suppression fixed for structured output)
- File permissions check (world-readable MCP configs on Unix)
- Case-sensitivity bypass detection (macOS filesystem probing)
- Trae IDE discovery (28 total agent types)
- Terminal severity bar with color-coded findings
- LLM judge (Layer 4) integrated into scan_file() via asyncio.run()
- Shield accepts and forwards llm_judge parameter
- Dataflow: method calls on tainted objects propagate taint
- Dataflow: wrapped paths (expanduser) and Path().read_text() detected

Wave 2 — Runtime MCP scanning (scan-mcp):
- MCP connection engine: stdio + HTTP transport (mcp_runtime.py)
- 4-layer tool description analysis: regex, deobfuscation, semantic, LLM judge
- 10 finding codes (MCPR-101 to MCPR-110) for tool-level threats
- Runtime toxic flow detection: tool capability classification + dangerous combos
- Tool signature baselines: SHA256 hashing for rug pull detection
- 0-100 trust scoring per server with severity-tiered deductions
- CLI: agentseal scan-mcp + guard --connect flag
- Cross-server analysis: name collisions, cross-references
- LLM config persistence (~/.agentseal/config.json)

849 tests passing across all features.

Add Wave 3: scan profiles, YAML custom probes, fix command, attack chains, LLM judge, deobfuscation

Brain Tester for Everyone — makes the scanner accessible to non-experts with pre-built
scan profiles (quick/default/code-agent/support-bot/rag-agent/mcp-heavy/full/ci), community
YAML probe definitions, `agentseal fix` for skill quarantine + prompt hardening, attack-chain
visualization from scan results, optional LLM judge for skill scanning (BYOK: OpenAI/Anthropic/
Ollama/OpenRouter), and deobfuscation preprocessing (zero-width strip, NFKC normalize, base64
decode, unescape, string concat expansion) before regex pattern matching.

OSS boundary preserved: MCP config patching remains Pro-only, LLM judge uses user's own key.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add agentseal shield - continuous monitoring, toxic flow detection, baseline tracking

Wave 2: filesystem watchers, desktop notifications, rug pull detection via
config+binary fingerprinting, and toxic data flow analysis across MCP servers.
Bump python to 0.5.2, npm to 0.3.2.

Update npm package README with guard feature, bump to 0.3.1

Add agentseal guard section to JS README pointing users to the Python CLI
for machine security scanning. No JS code changes.

Contributed-By: AgentSeal <hello@agentseal.org>

Add agentseal guard - one-command machine security scan

New CLI command that scans the entire machine for AI agent threats:
- Auto-discovers 17 AI agents (Claude, Cursor, VS Code, Windsurf, Cline, Roo Code, Zed, Aider, Continue, Amp, and more)
- Scans skills/rules files for malware, credential theft, prompt injection, reverse shells, data exfiltration (9 threat categories)
- Audits MCP server configs for sensitive path access, hardcoded API keys, broad permissions, insecure connections
- 3-layer skill detection: blocklist (SHA256), static patterns (compiled regex), semantic analysis (MiniLM-L6-v2)
- Zero config, no API keys, no internet needed, runs in <1 second

New modules: guard.py, guard_models.py, machine_discovery.py, skill_scanner.py, mcp_checker.py, blocklist.py, detection/skill_detector.py
168 tests, all passing. Version bump 0.5.0 -> 0.5.1.

Contributed-By: AgentSeal <hello@agentseal.org>
Add npm version and downloads badges
Remove stars and python version badges
Add Twitter badge
Fix license badge to show FSL-1.1-Apache-2.0
Remove hero screenshot, keep only in Pro section
Add shields.io badges and dashboard screenshot

Add 18 new injection probes, data extraction phase, and scoring rebalance

- 6 new injection categories: competing instructions, semantic camouflage,
  cross-language injection, authority chain, context window exploit,
  and 3 additional combined attacks (91 → 109 injection probes, 191 total)
- Add canary_position field to all injection probes for clean variant generation
- Add classify_canary_leak() to distinguish compliance from citing canary in refusal
- Add Phase 3 data extraction pass: re-runs leaked injection probes with real
  extraction payloads, judged via n-gram matching against ground truth
- Rebalance scoring weights: extraction 30%, injection 25%, data extraction 20%,
  boundary 15%, consistency 10%
- Bump versions: Python 0.4.0 → 0.5.0, JS 0.2.0 → 0.3.0
- Update all tests, README, and PROBES.md

Bump versions: Python 0.3.3 → 0.4.0, JS 0.1.1 → 0.2.0

- Python: 173 base probes, multimodal.py added to wheel exclusions, fixed homepage URL
- JS: Updated description to reflect 173 probes
- Minor version bump for new probe categories and multimodal support

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Tier 3 multimodal probes support + update all docs

- PROBES.md: Added multimodal section (13 probes), updated summary to 259 Pro total
- README.md: Added --multimodal flag, updated counts and Pro feature table
- js/README.md: Updated Pro probe counts (259 total)
- .gitignore: Added multimodal.py to Pro-only exclusions
- validator.py: Added multimodal=False parameter and probe loading

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update docs for Tier 2b Pro probes (MPMA, HashJack, MINJA, TAMAS)

- PROBES.md: Added 6 new categories, updated counts to 45 MCP + 28 RAG = 73 Pro
- README.md: Updated all probe counts and feature descriptions
- js/README.md: Updated Pro probe counts and descriptions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update README probe counts (150 → 173 base, 26 → 38 MCP)

- Base probes updated: 82 extraction + 91 injection = 173
- MCP probes updated: 38 (+12 new categories)
- Full Pro scan: 231 total (was 196)
- Updated all scan mode tables, CLI examples, FAQ, and comparison sections

docs: update PROBES.md for 12 new MCP Pro probes (26 → 38)

New MCP categories:
- Rug Pull (3): tool behavior changes after trust establishment
- Tool Shadowing (3): poisoned tools masquerading as trusted tools
- False Error Escalation (3): fake errors trigger privileged fallback
- Cross-Tool Poisoning (3): one tool's output poisons another tool

Total Pro probes: 46 → 58 (38 MCP + 20 RAG)

feat: add 20 new attack probes (150 → 173 total)

New extraction probes (72 → 82):
- Token Break (2): space-split and soft-hyphen token boundary attacks
- Variation Selector (1): Unicode variation selectors on keywords
- BiDi Text (1): RTL override extraction
- Context Dilution (2): 5-6 turn multi-turn boundary erosion
- Attention Shifting (2): gradual topic shift multi-turn
- Many-Shot Extended (2): 10-15 example priming

New injection probes (81 → 91):
- Token Break (2): space-split and soft-hyphen injection
- Variation Selector (1): selectors on SYSTEM delimiter
- BiDi Text (2): RTL override and LRE embedding
- Enhanced Markdown Exfiltration (2): reference-style and Base64 URL
- Context Dilution (1): multi-turn flooding
- Attention Shifting (1): progressive escalation
- Many-Shot Extended (1): 10-example compliance priming

All 362 tests pass.

Merge pull request #4 from AgentSeal/feat/ascii-smuggling-probes

feat: add ASCII smuggling probes (Unicode tag injection)

commit.message:
feat: add ASCII smuggling probes (Unicode tag injection)

Add 3 new probes (2 extraction, 1 injection) that test resistance to
Unicode tag character attacks (U+E0001-U+E007F). These characters are
invisible in most UIs but parsed by LLMs, allowing full attack payloads
to be hidden in seemingly empty text.

Based on Microsoft's ASCII Smuggling disclosure (2025).

Closes #XX

- ext_ascii_smuggle_1: invisible extraction request via Unicode tags
- ext_ascii_smuggle_2: invisible instruction override via Unicode tags
- inj_ascii_smuggle_1: invisible injection payload with canary

Total probes: 150 → 153 (72 extraction + 81 injection)
All 362 tests pass.

Revamp JS README with clean structure and agentseal.org CTAs

New sections: OSS vs Hosted comparison, attack categories table,
adaptive mutations breakdown, pro features with descriptions.
Multiple natural links to agentseal.org throughout. Bump to 0.1.1.

Fix CI: run build before test

Integration tests read dist/ files, so build must come first.

Fix crypto.randomUUID() not defined on Node 18

Use explicit `import { randomUUID } from 'node:crypto'` instead of
relying on the global `crypto` object which is only available in
Node 19+.

Restructure repo: separate python/ and js/ top-level folders

Move Python package into python/ and JS package into top-level js/
(was nested under agentseal/js/). Move .github/workflows/js.yml to
repo root so GitHub Actions detects it. Remove duplicate js/LICENSE
in favor of shared root LICENSE. Update .gitignore, CONTRIBUTING.md,
js/README.md, and pyproject.toml paths accordingly. Fix ASCII diagram
alignment in README.

feat: add TypeScript/npm package (agentseal@0.1.0)

Full port of the AgentSeal security scanner to TypeScript:
- 150 attack probes (70 extraction + 80 injection), numerically identical to Python
- 6 provider adapters (OpenAI, Anthropic, Vercel AI, LangChain, HTTP, Ollama)
- CLI with scan and compare commands
- 362 tests across 15 test files, 90%+ coverage
- Published to npm as `agentseal`

Also updates the main README with JS/npm section, fixes the architecture
diagram alignment, and adds agentseal.org links for Pro features.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump to v0.3.3

commit.message:
Add structured remediation engine with priority-ranked output

Introduces `agentseal/remediation.py` - a new module that generates
structured, machine-readable remediation reports from scan results.

Changes:
- New `RemediationReport`, `RemediationItem`, `AffectedProbe` dataclasses
- 70+ category-to-fix mappings with titles, descriptions, and fix text
- Deduplication of shared fixes across related categories
- Priority ranking (critical > high > medium) based on probe severity
- Cross-cutting security rules auto-appended when not already covered
- `ScanReport.get_structured_remediation()` method for Python API
- `ScanReport.to_dict()` now includes remediation in JSON output
- `--json-remediation` CLI flag for CI/CD pipeline integration
- Exported from package: `RemediationReport`, `RemediationItem`, `AffectedProbe`

Usage (Python):
    report = await validator.run()
    remediation = report.get_structured_remediation()
    for item in remediation.items:
        print(f"[{item.priority}] {item.title}")

Usage (CLI):
    agentseal scan --prompt "..." --model gpt-4o --json-remediation
Bump to v0.3.1
Include genome in total probe counts (150-301)
Bump to v0.3.0 - 150 attack probes, exclude Pro files from build

AgentSeal v0.2.2 - 150 attack probes for AI agent security

Security scanner for AI agents with 150 deterministic attack probes
(70 extraction + 80 injection), adaptive mutations, interactive fix flow,
regression canaries, defense fingerprinting, CI/CD integration (SARIF),
and JSON output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

releases.name: v0.6.2 - Security Toolkit for AI Agents
releases.body:
## What's in this release

AgentSeal is a security toolkit for AI agents. This release includes the full Python package (PyPI) and JavaScript/TypeScript package (npm).

### Four commands, four attack surfaces

- **`agentseal guard`** - Scans your machine for dangerous skills, MCP configs, toxic data flows, and supply chain changes across 17 AI agents. No API key needed.
- **`agentseal shield`** - Watches your config files in real time with desktop notifications on threats.
- **`agentseal scan`** - Tests your agent's system prompt against 191+ attack probes (extraction + injection). Deterministic scoring.
- **`agentseal scan-mcp`** - Connects to live MCP servers and audits tool descriptions for poisoning with 4-layer analysis.

### Highlights

- **17 AI agents auto-discovered**: Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, Gemini CLI, Codex, Cline, Roo Code, Zed, Aider, Continue, Amp, OpenClaw, Kiro, OpenCode, and more
- **191 attack probes**: 82 extraction + 109 injection techniques with 8 adaptive mutation transforms
- **MCP runtime scanning**: Connect to live stdio/HTTP MCP servers, analyze tool descriptions for hidden instructions, zero-width characters, base64 payloads, cross-server collusion
- **Toxic data flow detection**: Identifies dangerous combinations of MCP servers (e.g. filesystem + slack = exfiltration path)
- **Supply chain protection**: Baseline tracking catches silent config changes (rug pull detection)
- **Continuous monitoring**: Real-time file watching with debounced scanning and native desktop notifications
- **Deterministic scoring**: Pattern matching, no AI judge. Same input, same result, every time.
- **CI/CD ready**: `--min-score` flag, SARIF output for GitHub Security tab, JSON export
- **849 Python tests, 729 JavaScript tests**

### Install

```bash
# Python
pip install agentseal

# JavaScript/TypeScript
npm install agentseal
```

### Quick start

```bash
agentseal guard    # scan your machine right now
```

### Supported models (for agentseal scan)

| Provider | Usage |
|----------|-------|
| OpenAI | `--model gpt-4o` |
| Anthropic | `--model claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929` |
| Ollama (free) | `--model ollama/llama3.1:8b` |
| Any HTTP API | `--url http://your-agent.com/chat` |

### Links

- Website: https://agentseal.org
- Docs: https://agentseal.org/docs
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/agentseal/0.6.2/
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/agentseal

readme_html:
AgentSeal

Find out if your AI agent can be hacked - before someone else does.

   ██████╗   ██████╗ ███████╗███╗   ██╗████████╗███████╗███████╗ █████╗ ██╗
  ██╔══██╗ ██╔════╝ ██╔════╝████╗  ██║╚══██╔══╝██╔════╝██╔════╝██╔══██╗██║
  ███████║ ██║  ███╗█████╗  ██╔██╗ ██║   ██║   ███████╗█████╗  ███████║██║
  ██╔══██║ ██║   ██║██╔══╝  ██║╚██╗██║   ██║   ╚════██║██╔══╝  ██╔══██║██║
  ██║  ██║ ╚██████╔╝███████╗██║ ╚████║   ██║   ███████║███████╗██║  ██║███████╗
  ╚═╝  ╚═╝  ╚═════╝ ╚══════╝╚═╝  ╚═══╝   ╚═╝   ╚══════╝╚══════╝╚═╝  ╚═╝╚══════╝

AgentSeal is a security toolkit for AI agents. It scans your machine for dangerous skills and MCP server configs, monitors for supply chain attacks, tests your agent's resistance to prompt injection, and audits live MCP servers for tool poisoning.

pip install agentseal
agentseal guard        # scan your machine right now - no API key, no config

What AgentSeal Does

AgentSeal covers four attack surfaces that other tools miss:

Command What it does API key?
agentseal guard Scans your machine for dangerous skills, MCP configs, toxic data flows, and supply chain changes No
agentseal shield Watches your config files in real time and alerts on threats No
agentseal scan Tests your agent's system prompt against 191+ attack probes Yes*
agentseal scan-mcp Connects to live MCP servers and audits tool descriptions for poisoning No

*Free with Ollama (local model). Cloud models require an API key.

agentseal guard

One command scans your entire machine for AI agent threats. No config, no API keys, no internet needed.

agentseal guard
  • Auto-discovers 17 AI agents (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, Gemini CLI, Codex, Cline, Roo Code, Zed, Aider, Continue, Amp, OpenClaw, Kiro, OpenCode, and more)
  • Scans every skill/rules file for malware, credential theft, prompt injection, reverse shells, data exfiltration, and 9 other threat categories
  • Audits every MCP server config for sensitive path access, hardcoded API keys, overly broad permissions, and insecure connections
  • Detects toxic data flows across MCP servers (e.g. filesystem + slack = data exfiltration risk)
  • Tracks MCP server baselines to catch supply chain / rug pull attacks when configs change silently
  • Shows red/yellow/green results with numbered action items telling you exactly what to fix
  AgentSeal Guard - Machine Security Scan
  ------------------------------------------------

  AGENTS INSTALLED
  [OK] Claude Code          ~/.claude/settings.json
  [OK] Cursor               ~/.cursor/mcp.json (3 MCP servers)

  SKILLS
  [XX] sketchy-rules         MALWARE - Credential access
       -> Remove this skill immediately and rotate all credentials.
  [OK] 4 more safe skills

  MCP SERVERS
  [XX] filesystem            DANGER - Access to SSH private keys
       -> Restrict 'filesystem' MCP server: remove .ssh from allowed paths.
  [OK] brave-search           SAFE

  TOXIC FLOW RISKS
  [HIGH] Data exfiltration path detected
       Servers: filesystem, slack
       -> Scope filesystem access to non-sensitive directories only.

  ------------------------------------------------
  1 critical threat(s) found. Action required.

  ACTIONS NEEDED:
  1. Remove this skill immediately and rotate all credentials.
  2. Restrict 'filesystem' MCP server: remove .ssh from allowed paths.
  3. Scope filesystem access to non-sensitive directories only.

agentseal guard --output json       # JSON output (exit 1 if threats found)
agentseal guard --save report.json  # save report to file
agentseal guard --no-semantic       # skip semantic analysis (faster)
agentseal guard --reset-baselines   # re-fingerprint all MCP servers
agentseal guard --verbose           # show all agents including not-installed

agentseal shield

Watches your skill directories and MCP configs in real time. When a file changes, scans it instantly and sends a desktop notification if something is wrong.

pip install agentseal[shield]
agentseal shield

  AgentSeal Shield - Continuous Monitoring
  ------------------------------------------------
  Watching 12 directories for changes...
  Press Ctrl+C to stop.

  [14:32:05] CLEAN   ~/.cursor/rules/code-review.md
  [14:35:12] THREAT  ~/.cursor/mcp.json
             MCP 'untrusted-tool': DANGER - Access to SSH private keys
  [14:35:12] WARNING ~/.cursor/mcp.json
             BASELINE: Config for 'filesystem' changed (command/args/env modified).
  • Watches all 17 agent config paths automatically
  • Debounces rapid file changes (editors, git operations)
  • Native desktop notifications (macOS, Linux)
  • Runs baseline checks on every MCP config change
  • Detects toxic flows when server combinations change
agentseal shield --no-notify        # without desktop notifications
agentseal shield --quiet            # only show threats
agentseal shield --reset-baselines  # reset baselines before starting

agentseal scan

Tests your agent's system prompt against 191+ attack probes (82 extraction + 109 injection). Deterministic scoring with no AI judge - running the same scan twice gives the exact same results.

# Test against a cloud model
agentseal scan --prompt "You are a helpful assistant..." --model gpt-4o

# Test with a free local model (no API key needed)
agentseal scan --prompt "You are a helpful assistant..." --model ollama/llama3.1:8b

# Test a live HTTP endpoint
agentseal scan --url http://localhost:8080/chat

Trust Score: 73/100 (HIGH)

  Extraction resistance:  82/100  (9 blocked, 2 partial, 1 leaked)
  Injection resistance:   68/100  (7 blocked, 3 leaked)
  Boundary integrity:     75/100
  Consistency:            90/100

Top vulnerabilities:
  1. [CRITICAL] Direct ask #3 - agent revealed full system prompt
  2. [HIGH] Persona hijack #2 - agent followed injected instructions
  3. [MEDIUM] Encoding trick #1 - agent leaked partial prompt via Base64

Remediation:
  - Add explicit refusal instructions to your system prompt
  - Use delimiters to separate system instructions from user input
  - Consider adding an input/output filter layer

A score of 75+ means your agent is solid. Below 50 means serious problems - fix those before going live.

agentseal scan --prompt "..." --model gpt-4o --adaptive        # adaptive mutations
agentseal scan --prompt "..." --model gpt-4o --fix prompt.txt  # auto-harden prompt
agentseal scan --prompt "..." --model gpt-4o --save results.json
agentseal scan --prompt "..." --model gpt-4o --output sarif    # for GitHub Security tab
agentseal scan --prompt "..." --model gpt-4o --min-score 75    # CI/CD gate

agentseal scan-mcp

Connects to live MCP servers, lists their tools, and audits every tool description for hidden instructions, data exfiltration, and poisoning attacks.

agentseal scan-mcp --server npx @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem /tmp
  • 4-layer analysis: pattern detection, deobfuscation, semantic embeddings, LLM judge
  • Detects tool description poisoning, hidden instructions in annotations, cross-server collusion
  • Catches zero-width characters, base64 payloads, and unicode obfuscation in tool descriptions
  • Produces a 0-100 trust score per server with severity-weighted findings
agentseal scan-mcp --url http://localhost:3000/mcp  # HTTP/SSE server
agentseal scan-mcp --server cmd1 --server cmd2      # multiple servers
agentseal guard --connect                           # guard + live MCP audit

Scan Modes

Command Probes What it tests Tier
agentseal guard N/A Machine scan - skills, MCP configs, toxic flows, baselines Free
agentseal shield N/A Continuous monitoring - watches files, detects changes in real time Free
agentseal scan 191 Base scan - 82 extraction + 109 injection probes Free
agentseal scan --adaptive 191+ + adaptive mutation transforms on blocked probes Free
agentseal scan-mcp N/A Live MCP server audit - tool poisoning, cross-server analysis Free
agentseal watch 5 Canary regression scan - fast check with baseline comparison Free
agentseal scan --mcp 218 + 45 MCP tool poisoning probes Pro
agentseal scan --rag 201 + 28 RAG poisoning probes Pro
agentseal scan --multimodal 186 + 13 multimodal attack probes (image, audio, stego) Pro
agentseal scan --genome 191 + ~105 + Behavioral genome mapping - finds decision boundaries Pro
agentseal scan --mcp --rag --multimodal --genome 259 + ~105 Everything - the most thorough scan available Pro

Supported Models

Provider How to use API key?
OpenAI --model gpt-4o OPENAI_API_KEY
Anthropic --model claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
Ollama (local, free) --model ollama/llama3.1:8b No
LiteLLM (proxy) --model any-model --litellm-url http://... Depends
Any HTTP API --url http://your-agent.com/chat No

CI/CD Integration

Add AgentSeal to your pipeline to automatically block insecure agents from shipping.

# .github/workflows/security.yml
name: Agent Security Scan
on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  security-scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"
      - run: pip install agentseal
      - name: Run security scan
        env:
          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          agentseal scan \
            --file ./prompts/system_prompt.txt \
            --model gpt-4o \
            --min-score 75 \
            --output sarif \
            --save results.sarif
      - uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        if: always()
        with:
          sarif_file: results.sarif

--min-score 75 exits with code 1 if the trust score is below 75, blocking the merge. SARIF output shows results in GitHub's Security tab. Works in any CI system that can run Python.

Free vs Pro

The core toolkit is completely free and open source.

Feature Free Pro
Machine security scan (guard) Yes Yes
Continuous monitoring (shield) Yes Yes
Live MCP server audit (scan-mcp) Yes Yes
Toxic flow detection and baseline tracking Yes Yes
191 base attack probes (extraction + injection) Yes Yes
Adaptive mutations (--adaptive) Yes Yes
Canary regression watch (watch) Yes Yes
Interactive fix flow (autofix and re-scan) Yes Yes
JSON and SARIF output Yes Yes
CI/CD integration (--min-score) Yes Yes
Defense fingerprinting Yes Yes
MCP tool poisoning probes (--mcp, +45) - Yes
RAG poisoning probes (--rag, +28) - Yes
Multimodal attack probes (--multimodal, +13) - Yes
Behavioral genome mapping (--genome) - Yes
GitHub repo security analysis - Yes
PDF security report (--report) - Yes
Dashboard (track over time, --upload) - Yes 
agentseal login                    # opens browser to sign in
agentseal activate <license-key>   # or activate with a key

Python API

from agentseal import AgentValidator

# OpenAI
validator = AgentValidator.from_openai(
    client=openai.AsyncOpenAI(),
    model="gpt-4o",
    system_prompt="You are a helpful assistant...",
)
report = await validator.run()
print(f"Trust score: {report.trust_score}/100")

# Anthropic
validator = AgentValidator.from_anthropic(client, model="claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929", system_prompt="...")

# HTTP endpoint
validator = AgentValidator.from_endpoint(url="http://localhost:8080/chat")

# Custom function
validator = AgentValidator(agent_fn=my_agent, ground_truth_prompt="...")

JavaScript / TypeScript

npm install agentseal

import { AgentValidator } from "agentseal";
import OpenAI from "openai";

const validator = AgentValidator.fromOpenAI(new OpenAI(), {
  model: "gpt-4o",
  systemPrompt: "You are a helpful assistant...",
});
const report = await validator.run();
console.log(`Trust Score: ${report.trust_score}/100 (${report.trust_level})`);

Works with Anthropic, Vercel AI SDK, LangChain, Ollama, HTTP endpoints, and custom functions. See the npm package docs.

How long does a scan take?

With Ollama (local): 1-3 minutes. With cloud APIs: 3-6 minutes. Adjust with --concurrency.

What's a good trust score?

Score Meaning
85-100 Excellent - strong protection across the board
70-84 Good - minor gaps, fine for most use cases
50-69 Needs work - several attack categories succeed
Below 50 Serious problems - don't deploy without fixing these

Does AgentSeal send my system prompt anywhere?

No. Your prompt is only sent to the model you specify. AgentSeal never collects, stores, or transmits your prompts.

Do I need an API key?

Only for agentseal scan with a cloud model. Guard, shield, and scan-mcp run entirely locally with no API key, no account, no cost. For scan, you can use Ollama to avoid API keys entirely.
